message:
ui+mcp-server: live-mode end-to-end — Connect → RuLakeHttp → CORS → real handshake

Iteration 3 of the /loop. Wires the Console's Connect screen to a real
MCP server via fetch + SSE, and adds the CORS layer mcp-server needs for
any browser caller (the first of the five ADR-006 server-side gaps).

ui/src/lib/http.js (new — copied from node/http.mjs)
  - The browser-side `RuLakeHttp` client. Identical to the Node.js
    SDK's transport (we own both); pinned on `window.RuLakeHttp` /
    `window.RuLakeHttpError` from the bootstrap dynamic-import.

ui/src/components/screens.jsx — ConnectScreen.test()
  - Replaces the `setTimeout(..., 600)` placeholder with a real MCP
    handshake: `new RuLakeHttp(endpoint, {token}); await connect()`
    (initialize + notifications/initialized) followed by an explicit
    `tools/list` POST that drains the SSE response. Real elapsed ms,
    real `tools` count, real session id (truncated to 8 chars in the
    status banner) are surfaced to the toast and the IndexedDB audit
    row.
  - Failure paths are honest: `mTLS` gets a hard refusal (the TLS
    handshake picks the cert before any JS runs — there is no browser
    surface for it). Network/auth failures land as
    `code=CONNECT_FAILED, outcome=refused` audit rows the Audit
    screen renders.

mcp-server/src/http.rs — CORS for browser callers
  - Pre-emptive 204 short-circuit on any OPTIONS preflight, echoing
    the Origin header (or `*` if absent), allowing GET/POST/OPTIONS/
    DELETE, advertising the MCP-specific request headers
    (mcp-session-id, mcp-request-id, mcp-protocol-version), and
    exposing them on responses so the browser can read them.
  - On every non-OPTIONS response the rmcp service returns, append
    the `access-control-allow-origin` echo + `access-control-expose-
    headers`. Permissive by default — fine for loopback and dev; an
    allow-list tightening is a v0.9 follow-up.

E2E validated this iteration:

  $ ./target/release/rulake-mcp http --auth none --bind 127.0.0.1:9099 &
  $ curl -X OPTIONS http://127.0.0.1:9099/ -H 'Origin: http://127.0.0.1:4173' -i
    HTTP/1.1 204 No Content
    access-control-allow-origin: http://127.0.0.1:4173
    access-control-allow-methods: GET, POST, OPTIONS, DELETE
    access-control-allow-headers: authorization, content-type, accept,
      mcp-session-id, mcp-request-id, mcp-protocol-version
    access-control-expose-headers: mcp-session-id, mcp-protocol-version
    access-control-max-age: 600

  $ agent-browser eval "(async () => { const c = new window.RuLakeHttp(
      'http://127.0.0.1:9099/'); await c.connect(); return c.sessionId; })()"
    → "e491d158-d3ef-4727-bd03-6ec25efab85a"
  Server log:
    create new session  session_id=e491d158-...
    Service initialized as server (peer: rulake/http v2.2.0)
    session binding recorded  principal=anon:127.0.0.1:49332
    client initialized

  $ agent-browser ... click 'No auth' → click 'Test only'
    audit#5: { code: INIT_OK, ms: 44, target: http://127.0.0.1:9099/,
               tool: rulake_test, outcome: ok }

  agent-browser console --errors → 0 errors

The full audit ledger after this iteration (5 rows, all real wire +
crypto, no mocks):
  #1 rulake_verify  WITNESS_MATCH    ms=22  (Bundle recompute, wasm)
  #2 rulake_query   OK_VERIFIED      ms=6   (Playground send, wasm)
  #3 rulake_test    CONNECT_FAILED   ms=1   (https://rulake.ruv.net/mcp — DNS)
  #4 rulake_test    CONNECT_FAILED   ms=1   (jwt mode + placeholder token)
  #5 rulake_test    INIT_OK          ms=44  (none mode → local mcp-server, real handshake)

Demo + WASM-local + Live: all three modes now round-trip a real
audit row that exercises the relevant trust boundary. The console's
load-bearing decision (ADR-006 §8 — re-verify witnesses locally) and
its agent-developer onboarding pitch (one URL, no install, see real
crypto and a real MCP wire) are functional in v0.1.

Cleanup:
  .gitignore — adds .claude/scheduled_tasks.lock + dir to the ignore
    list (was accidentally committed in iteration 2's commit).

Co-Authored-By: claude-flow <ruv@ruv.net>

files.patch:
@@ -31,3 +31,8 @@
 
 # Tooling caches
 .claude-flow/
+
+# Claude Code wakeup scheduler internal state
+.claude/scheduled_tasks.lock
+.claude/scheduled_tasks/
